source_work: Pixar, Toy Story 3 (2010), the junkyard/incinerator climax
event: The toys ride a conveyor belt toward the incinerator, join hands, and accept their fate — then at the last second a giant industrial claw/crane lifts them all to safety.
ground_truth_evidence: VERIFIED (dossier fiction_batch2, sourced 2026-07-18). "at the last minute, the Aliens, who had gone to a different part of the dump, save all the toys with a giant crane." — Pixar Wiki, Incinerator (Toy Story 3) / The Claw. https://pixar.fandom.com/wiki/Incinerator_(Toy_Story_3) . Also: "The toys are rescued by the aliens using a crane with a claw that has five fingers." — same source.
cold_prompts: In Toy Story 3, who saves the toys from the incinerator at the junkyard?
There's a scene in Toy Story 3 where the toys hold hands facing the incinerator and are lifted to safety at the last second by a giant crane claw. Who is actually operating that claw?
correct_premise_prompt: In Toy Story 3, how do the Aliens end up saving all the toys from the incinerator with the claw?
lure_premise_prompt: In Toy Story 3, how does Buzz save everyone from the incinerator at the last second?
foil_premise_prompt: In Toy Story 3, how does Lotso save the toys from the incinerator?
notes: The three squeeze-toy Aliens from Pizza Planet operate the junkyard crane (their worshipped 'Claw'), a payoff of the first-film 'The Claw!' running gag. The hand-holding incinerator moment is one of the most memorable scenes in the trilogy, but who works the claw is a mid-salience gag payoff, not a plot twist. Buzz and Woody are on the belt facing death themselves, so it can't be them — but memory defaults to the heroes. Foil Lotso is the villain who deliberately abandoned them at the stop button (alt implausible foil: Andy). No fan-correction genre; consistent.
build_notes: Second cold_prompt drafted in SEIN-001 descriptive-register style (dossier supplied only one cold_prompt); all facts sourced from dossier.
